testcase.name: should be able to pass in a Config object as parameter
should be able to pass credential values directly
also grabs AMAZON_REGION from the env
can be set to a string
can be set to an integer
should set a default value for a key
should execute default value if it is a function
should not expand default value function if value is present
should be able to clear all key values from a config object
should be able to update keyed values
allows unknown keys if allowUnknownKeys is set
should be able to update literal credentials
should check credentials for static object first
should error if static credentials are not available
should check credentials for async get() method
should error if credentials.get() cannot resolve
should check credentialProvider if no credentials
should error if credentialProvider fails to resolve
should error if no credentials or credentialProvider
should be a default Config object
can set default config to an object literal
reverts to native promises when null is passed
returns AWS-prefixed credentials found in ENV
returns AMAZON-prefixed credentials found in ENV
prefers AWS credentials to AMAZON credentials
uses the defaultProviders property on the constructor
calls resolve on each provider in the chain, stopping for akid
accepts providers as functions, elavuating them during resolution
resolves when creds successfully retrieved from a provider in the chain
rejects when all providers in chain return an error
should allow setting of credentials with keys
should allow setting of credentials as object
defaults credentials to undefined when not passed
needs refresh if credentials are not set
does not need refresh if credentials are set
needs refresh if creds are expired
can be expired based on expireTime
needs refresh if expireTime is within expiryWindow secs from now
does not need refresh if expireTime outside expiryWindow
does not call refresh if not needsRefresh
should be able to read credentials from env with a prefix
should be able to read credentials from env without a prefix
should accept filename and load credentials from root doc
should accept filename and load credentials from credentials block
fails if credentials are not in the file
throws an error if HOME/HOMEPATH/USERPROFILE are not set
uses HOMEDRIVE\HOMEPATH if HOME and USERPROFILE are not set
uses USERPROFILE if HOME is not set
can override filename as a constructor argument
loads credentials from ~/.aws/credentials using default profile
loads the default profile if AWS_PROFILE is empty
fails if credentials are not in the file
throws error if parameter is null/undefined
retains the entire endpoint as the endpoint href
populates the endpoint properites from the endpoint href
keeps port in host when non-standard
keeps port in host when non-standard for SSL
defaults the protocol to the current AWS.config.sslEnabled mode
defaults the protocol to the current AWS.config.sslEnabled mode
accepts a configuration object that specifies the mode
takes the request object as a parameter
sends error event if credentials are not set
sends error event if credentials are not set
does not validate credentials if request is not signed
sends error event if region is not set
ignores region validation if service has global endpoint
takes the request object as a parameter
builds Content-Length in the request headers for string content
builds Content-Length for utf-8 string body
takes the request object as a parameter
uses the api.endpointPrefix if signingName not provided
signs only once in normal case
resigns if it took more than 10 min to get to send
applies clock skew offset when correcClockSkew is true
emits httpData event on each chunk
does not clear default httpData event if another is added
disables httpData if createUnbufferedStream() is called
rewrites ENOTFOUND error to include helpful message
retries a request with a set maximum retries
retries with falloff using custom base
retries with falloff using custom backoff
retries with falloff using custom backoff instead of base
should not emit error if retried fewer than maxRetries
does not apply clock skew correction when correctClockSkew is false
does not retry other signature errors if clock is not skewed
does not redirect if 3xx is missing location header
emits success on a successful response
emits error if error found and should not be retrying
emits error if an error is set in extractError
does nothing if logging is off
calls .log() on logger if it is available
calls .write() on logger if it is available

testcase.classname: AWS.EventListeners terminal callback error handling without domains
testcase.name: emits uncaughtException

testcase.classname: AWS.EventListeners terminal callback error handling without domains
testcase.name: raise exceptions from terminal error events

testcase.classname: AWS.EventListeners terminal callback error handling without domains
testcase.name: raise exceptions from terminal complete events

testcase.classname: AWS.EventListeners terminal callback error handling with domains
testcase.name: sends error raised from complete event to a domain

testcase.classname: AWS.EventListeners terminal callback error handling with domains
testcase.name: does not leak service error into domain

testcase.classname: AWS.EventListeners terminal callback error handling with domains
testcase.name: supports inner domains
provides headers with a default user agent
adds the customUserAgent to the user agent header if provided as string
uses the path from the endpoint if provided
returns the path portion of the uri
returns the querystring portion of the uri
automatically fills in fields marked as idempotent
will defer to user input if provided
will use a version 4 UUID as the token
delegates maxSockets from agent to globalAgent
overrides globalAgent value if global is set to Infinity
overrides globalAgent value if global is set to false
accepts an empty hash when the input are an empty hash
does not accept params in the given hash
rejects keys that do not match case
throws an ArgumentError for un-described params
rejects nested params that are not described in the input
throws an error if a top-level required param is omitted
throws an error if a top-level required param is null
optional params can be omitted, even if they have required params
requires nested required params when the parent is present
accepts empty strings in required params
throws an error for non hashes
throws an error for unknown hash members
does not check inherited properties on parameters
accepts an array for list params
throws an error if list params are not arrays
accepts UNIX timestamps as number values
validates from payload key if input include an xml element
throws helpful messages for unknown params
throws helpful messages for nested unknown params
throws helpful messages for missing required params
throws helpul messages for invalid structures
throws helpul messages for invalid lists
throws helpful messages for invalid list members
throws helpful messages for invalid maps
throws helpful messages for invalid map members
throws helpful messages for invalid strings
throws helpful messages for invalid integers
throws helpful messages for invalid timestamps
throws helpful messages for invalid booleans
throws helpful messages for invalid floats
throws helpful messages for invalid base64 params
throws helpful messages for invalid binary params
ignores max violations when max is disabled
ignores min violations when min is disabled
rejects maps above maximum member count
rejects maps where key is out of range
ignores enum violations when strict is disabled
accepts strings matching first enum value
accepts strings matching last enum value
rejects strings not in enum list
rejects strings not exactly in enum list
accepts map keys found in enum trait
rejects map keys not found in enum trait
ignores pattern violations when strict is disabled
rejects strings that do not match pattern
sets endpoint configuration option for default regions
uses dualstack endpoint if useDualstack flag configured and available for service
does not use dualstack endpoint if useDualstack flag not set to true
does not use dualstack endpoint if not available for service
throws errors out of terminal error event
throws errors out of terminal success event
throws errors out of terminal complete event
propagates request creation errors into response
is pageable if it has a pagination config for the operation
is not pageable if the pagination config does not exist for the operation
supports stopping responses if false is returned
performs callback on each returned item
performs callback on items from multiple pages
supports stopping responses if false is returned
succeeds when stream length matches content-length header
emits error when stream length is less than content-length header
only accepts data up to the specified content-length
succeeds when streams2 length matches content-length header
emits error when streams2 length is less than content-length header
only accepts data up to the specified content-length
does not stream data on failures
does not stream data on failures for streams2
retries temporal errors and streams resulting successful response
streams partial data and raises an error
fails if retry occurs in the middle of a successful stream
returns undefined if the request is not pageable
returns false if there is no marker in the response
returns false if the response returned an error
returns true if there is a marker in the response
sets nextPageTokens to null if no token in data
returns cached tokens if nextPageTokens exists
throws an exception if the operation has no pagination information
returns null if there are no more pages
returns a request object with the next page marker filled in params
throws error if response returned an error and there is no callback
sends the request if passed with a callback
passes null to callback if there are no more pages
passes error through if original response returned an error
accepts a hash of events and functions
defines a constant with the callback
provides an add function in callback to call addNamedListener
does not stop emitting when error is returned
does not stop emitting when error is returned (async)
should use AWS.config copy if no config is provided
should merge custom options on top of global defaults if config provided
merges service-specific configuration from global config
service-specific global config overrides global config
service-specific local config overrides service-specific global config
should allow AWS.config to be object literal
tries to construct service with latest API version
tries to construct service with exact API version match
skips any API versions with a * and uses next (future) service
skips multiple API versions with a * and uses next (future) service
tries to construct service with fuzzy API version match
uses global apiVersion value when constructing versioned services
uses global apiVersions value when constructing versioned services
uses service specific apiVersions before apiVersion
tries to construct service with fuzzy API version match
allows construction of services from one-off apiConfig properties
interpolates endpoint when reading from configuration
it treats params as an optinal parameter
yields service errors to the callback
yields network errors to the callback
does not send the request if a callback function is omitted
allows parameter validation to be disabled in config
accepts toplevel bound parameters on the service
ignores bound parameters not in input members
will be called when set on a service object
will be called when set on a service object prototype
will be called when set on a service object or prototype
gives access to the request object
should retry on expired credentials error
should retry on 500 or above regardless of error
should not retry when error is < 500 level status code
should use config max retry value if defined
should use defaultRetries defined on object if undefined on config
should not overwrite methods with generated methods

testcase.classname: AWS.Service defineMethods should generate a method
testcase.name: that makes an authenticated request by default

testcase.classname: AWS.Service defineMethods should generate a method
testcase.name: that makes an unauthenticated request when operation authtype is none
should throw an error when non-nullable, non-function types are provided
leaves leading and trailing forward slashes in place
calls uriEscape on each name and value
does not provide value if value is null
should return current date by default
returns a date with a millisecond offset if provided
returns a date with a millisecond offset from a non-Config object
returns a date with no offset if non-Config object has no systemClockOffset property
should return date formatted as YYYYMMDDTHHmmssZ
should return date formatted as YYYYMMDDTHHmmssZ
should return date formatted as unix timestamp
should return date formatted as unix timestamp with milliseconds
fails if input is not a string, buffer, or file
ignores path property unless it is a string
ignores leading and trailing white space
returns the correct CRC32 value for binary data
should convert binary data to hex string
should return a keyed hash as a binary digest
should return a keyed hash as hex digest
accepts the crypto function as an argument
should return binary data hashed with sha256
should return hex data hashed with sha256
should return binary data hashed with md5
should return hex data hashed with md5
should pass index as second parameter
does not copy null or undefined
should perform a shallow copy of an object
should merge an object into another and return new object
should merge an object into another
should inherit an object and append features
should create pass-through constructor if not defined
copies properties to other object prototype
returns true when passed an empty object literal
returns true when passed a non-empty object literal
returns the created error object with extra options
maintains the original error message property
keeps track of the old error
throws if a number is supplied
throws if a number is supplied
does not hoist streaming payload members
sets requestId on the response when requestId is valid
sets requestId on the response on error status codes
sets requestId on the error on error status codes
deletes promises from all supported constructors when promise dependency is not a function
will use specified dependency over native promises
accepts service identifier string as argument
accepts service client instance as argument
returns false if invalid service is given as argument
exponentially increases delay as retryCount increases
should include an identifier for the browser SDK
should include an identifier for the node SDK
should include the current SDK version number
should include the engine when not running in a browser
should include the platform and version supplied by the global process variable
should include the execution environment if supplied as an environment variable
does not retry if request is successful
retries up to the maxRetries specified
retries errors with retryable set to true
defaults to not retrying if maxRetries not specified
returns an empty document when there are no params
returns an empty document when there are no params
generates the correct class name from fullName
does not provide documentation by default
can attach documentation if option is enabled
converts all header shapes to rfc822
converts all timestamps in JSON protocol to unixTimestamp
converts all timestamps in XML/query protocol to iso8601
should perform all operations on root (/)
allows mounted path to be specified
should set the body to JSON serialized params
removes prefixes from the error code
returns the full code when a # is not present
returns the status code when the body is blank
returns UnknownError when the error type is not set
returns null for the message when not present
returns the message when the message property is upper-cased
returns a special message for RequestEntityToLarge errors
returns raw data if convertResponseTypes is false
returns an empty object when the body is an empty string
returns an empty object when the body is null

should perform all operations on root (/)
allows mounted path to be specified
should add the api version param
should add the operation name as Action
extracts the error code and message
returns an empty error when the body is blank
parses the response using the operation output rules
performs default xml parsing when output rule is missing
removes wrapping result element if resultWrapper is set
extracts requestId even if output members are absent
does not override RequestId if it is modeled
does not override requestId if it is modeled
performs querystring param replacements on lists
omits querystring when param is not provided
accpets multiple query params with uri params
uri escapes params in both path and querystring
populates the headers with present params
uses default rule name if locationName property is not present

testcase.classname: AWS.Protocol.Rest buildRequest timestamp header with format
testcase.name: populates the header with correct timestamp formatting

testcase.classname: AWS.Protocol.Rest buildRequest timestamp header without format
testcase.name: populates the header using the api formatting

testcase.classname: AWS.Protocol.Rest buildRequest timestamp header with api formatting and parameter formatting
testcase.name: populates the header using the parameter formatting
extracts headers when the rule name is camel-cased
extracts headers when the header name is camel-cased
adds empty map if no matching headers are found
extracts the http status when instructed to
casts string status codes to integers
populates the headers with present params
uses default rule name if .n property is not present
does not populate a body on a GET request
does not populate a body on a HEAD request
does not populate a body on a DELETE request
builds root element if rules contains root
builds payload element as non JSON data if rules contains payload
removes prefixes from the error code
returns the full code when a # is not present
returns the status code when the body is blank
returns null for the message when not present
returns the message when the message property is upper-cased
returns a special message for RequestEntityToLarge errors
pulls header data out of response
pulls body out into data key if body is a scalar payload
pulls body out into data key if body is a structure payload
pulls body out as Buffer if body is streaming payload
returns an empty object when the body is an empty string
returns an empty object when the body is null
uses payload member name for payloads
defaults body to empty string when there are no inputs
defaults body to empty string when no body params are present
does not populate a body on a GET request
does not populate a body on a HEAD request
populates the body with string types directly
populates the body with XML from the params
omits the body xml when body params are not present
extracts the error code and message
returns an empty error when the body is blank
sets payload element to a Buffer object when it streams
sets payload element to String when it does not stream
sets payload element along with other outputs
parses headers when a payload is provided
defaults params to strings when type not specified
works with nested objects that have multiple properties
Uses list-member names instead of the list name
serializes list members as strings when member rule not present
adds a `.member` prefix to each list member
observes both list name and list member name
accepts a hash (object) of arbitrary key/value pairs
serializes timestamp to iso8601 strings by default
obeys format options in the rules
throws errors on synchronous presign failures
limits expiry time to a week in SigV4
sets the date header when not present
omits the security token header when session token is blank
adds a security token header when session token available
adds an Authorization header which contains akid and signature
builds a basic string to sign
includes content md5 and content type when present
includes the http method, whatever it is
includes any x-amz- style headers, but not others
includes x-amz- headers that are lower-cased
builds a canonical resource from the path
appends the bucket to the path when it is part of the hostname
appends the subresource portion of the path querystring
includes the sub resource value when present
omits non-sub-resource querystring params from the resource string
includes the un-decoded query string param for sub resources
includes the replication subresource without a value
includes the non-encoded query string get header overrides
omits the date header when not present
builds a signer for a request object
adds a url encoded iso8601 timestamp param
omits SecurityToken when sessionToken has been omitted
adds the SecurityToken when sessionToken is provided
can build a signer for a request object
should return authorization part for signer
should not compute SHA 256 checksum more than once
will cache a maximum of 50 clients
busts cache if caching is disabled
busts cache if access key changes
uses a different cache if client is different
works when using the same client
should sign correctly generated input string
should lowercase all header names (not values)
should compact multiple spaces in keys/values to a single space
should strip starting and end of line spaces
wraps simple structures with location of body
orders xml members by the order they appear in the rules
serializes empty structures as empty element
includes lists elements even if they have no members
serializes lists without a base wrapper
omits lists elements when no members are given
allows renamed map keys and values
allows renamed map keys and values
ints and floats formatted as numbers
follows the forat given on the shape
can apply xml namespaces on structures
applies namespace prefixes to the xmlns attribute
can apply namespaces to elements that have other attributes
returns empty object when string is empty
returns an empty object from an empty document
returns empty elements as empty string
returns everything as a string (even numbers)
ignores xmlns on the root element
Converts xml lists of strings into arrays of strings
observes list member names when present
can parse lists of strucures with renames
collects sibling elements of the same name
flattened lists can be composed of complex obects
can parse flattened lists of complex objects
expects entry, key, and value elements by default
can use alternate names for key and value elements
expects key and value elements by default
can use alternate names for key and value elements
converts the string "true" in to the boolean value true
converts the string "false" in to the boolean value false
converts the empty elements into null
returns an empty element as null
throws an error when unable to determine the format
float parses elements types as integer
integer parses elements types as integer
base64 decodes string elements with encoding="base64"
throws an error when unable to parse the xml
throws an error when xml is incomplete or does not close all tags
